title: Limit option on hierarchicalMenu behaving differently with multi values for single level
body:
**Do you want to request a *feature* or report a *bug*?**

Bug/Unexpected behavior/Documentation

**Bug: What is the current behavior?**

Here's a demo: https://codesandbox.io/s/93ovzv79vo

When the hierarchical menu is configured with `limit 2`, we get:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/123822/37028385-642c2ccc-2134-11e8-9246-92e44de1e9b8.png)

When the hierarchical menu is configured with `limit: 3`, we get:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/123822/37028407-74051320-2134-11e8-9d52-c9a2b5b59bfb.png)

As you can see, inside lvl0 there are actually more than 1 value but it only shows when we ask for `limit: 3`.

It does that because of the shape of the index and objects inside it, for every level we have multiple values instead of a single one:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/123822/37028449-915a166e-2134-11e8-95f3-bd508b86fcb2.png)

When you have a single value, it's behaving correctly, as seen in dev-novel: https://community.algolia.com/instantsearch.js/v2/dev-novel/?selectedStory=HierarchicalMenu.default

**Bug: What is the expected behavior?**

When using `limit: 2` if there are more than two values, they should be shown.

**Bug: What is the proposed solution?**

Either we fix the bug, or we update the documentation about multiple values per level and the fact that you need to pass down a bigger value. This issue was triggered by a user on support.

**What is the version you are using? Always use the latest one before opening a bug issue.**

Latest
